Notti in Rifugio
Fanes Traverse Cinque Torri Sorapis Ra Valles Croda da Lago Fonda-Savio Lagazuoi
Overnight Hikes · The Dolomites · Summer 2026

Walk all day. Sleep on the mountain. Wake to the enrosadira.

Full days on the trail, a hut each night, and the pale rock turning rose-gold at dawn — after the day crowds have gone home. Every route here is walking: no ropes, no cables, no head for heights required.

A hut-to-hut traverse + day-hikes Cable-free Self-guided

For Steve & Leigh — from Jimmi.

A note before you go

Steve, Leigh — a little something before your first nights up in the mountains. You haven't done this before; I have, for years, and I promise the huts are the best part of the Dolomites. Everything here is walking — no ropes, no nerve required — and the picks up top come from your family, my mom included, who's been hiking these valleys for decades. Start early, take a long lunch on the trail, and don't skip the strudel.

— Jimmi

How to read this guide

Every route is walking, not mountaineering — nothing needs cables, gear, or a head for heights. These are proper full days: start early, lunch on the trail, roll into the rifugio mid-afternoon.

Moderate

A steady day on a good path. A lift may give a leg up only where a real walk still follows — never straight to the top.

Confident

A big, satisfying day — more distance and climb. Still just a trail, no exposure. This is where the long hut-to-hut days sit.

Quiet Some Busy

A crowd rating on every route — late July / August is the busiest window of the year.

The best trick against the crowds

Sleep over. Day-trippers flood in around 10–11 and are gone by five — so as hut guests you get the place, and the dawn and dusk light, nearly to yourselves. On the busy routes: walk in late, out early, and weekdays beat weekends.

Book now — peak season

These weeks fill months ahead. It's July: reserve the huts you want this week. Full order at the bottom.

In your pack for a hut night

  • Sleeping-bag liner
  • Cash (euros)
  • Earplugs
  • Headlamp
  • Warm layer
  • Indoor slippers
  • Small towel
Your picks

The ones your family chose

Led by the places you and your mom know first-hand — a lifetime of local knowledge beats any review site. This is where I'd start.

01 Cortina / Padola 1–3 nights · pick below Confident Quiet ★

The Fanes Traversethe full-day hut-to-hut, on the gentlest high ground in the range

This is the trip. From your Ra Stua pick you cross the rolling Fanes–Sennes plateau — the calm, grassy, almost-empty Dolomites — finishing on the great Lagazuoi terrace. Full days, a new hut each evening, no crowds and no exposure the whole way. Pick the length that suits you:

Nights
1
Distance
~11 km
Ascent
+500 m
Highest
2 116 m
Lifts
None
Day1

Ra Stua → Rifugio Sennes

Shuttle to Ra Stua, up Val Salata to the plateau, and settle in at Sennes — the afternoon free to wander to Lago di Fòses or Rifugio Biella.

Moderate~5.5 km+500 m~2 hNight → Sennes
Day2

Sennes → Ra Stua

Back down via Fodara Vedla — gentle and mostly downhill — to the shuttle. A relaxed first taste of hut life.

Easy~7 km~2½ h

The gentle taster — one quiet night on the plateau, no big commitment.

Nights
2
Distance
~30 km
Total climb
~2 100 m
Highest
2 752 m
Lifts
Ride down
Day1

Ra Stua → Sennes → Rifugio Fanes / Lavarella

The plateau's two gentle legs rolled into one big, satisfying day, west across the heart of the park.

Confident~15–16 km+1 000–1 080 m~5½–6½ hNight → Fanes
Day2

Fanes → Scotoni → Lagazuoi

Down to Rifugio Scotoni and past Lago di Lagazuoi, then the cable-free bypass up to the Lagazuoi terrace for sunset.

Confident~14–15 km+1 100 m6–7 hNight → Lagazuoi
Day3

Ride down

Sunrise from the terrace, then the Lagazuoi cable car down — the trip's only lift, at the very end.

EasyCable car

Fewer, fuller days — the same huts and scenery as the 3-night, one night lighter.

Nights
3
Distance
~30 km
Total climb
~2 100 m
Highest
2 752 m
Lifts
Ride down
Day1

Ra Stua → Rifugio Sennes

Shuttle to Ra Stua, up Val Salata to the plateau. For a fuller day, loop via Lago di Fòses and Rifugio Biella before dropping to Sennes. One steady 40-min climb; the rest is gentle, cable-free plateau.

Moderate~6–11 km+500–700 m2–5 hNight → Sennes
Day2

Sennes → Rifugio Fanes / Lavarella

A proper plateau day west across the heart of the park — big skies, grazing meadows, larch and limestone. Non-technical throughout.

Confident~10–11 km~4–5 hNight → Fanes / Lavarella
Day3

Fanes → Scotoni → walk up to Lagazuoi

Down to Rifugio Scotoni and past Lago di Lagazuoi, then the cable-free bypass up to the Lagazuoi terrace (2,752 m) for the finest sunset in the range. Take the Scotoni line, not the direct Forcella del Lago gully.

Confident~14–15 km+1 100 m6–7 hNight → Lagazuoi
Day4

Ride down

Sunrise from the terrace, then the Lagazuoi cable car straight down to Passo Falzarego — the trip's only lift, at the very end.

EasyCable car~3 min

The full, unhurried version — a night on each plateau, the classic line.

Getting there

Start at Malga Ra Stua — its road is closed to cars 6 Jul – 6 Sep 2026, so take the €10 shuttle from Fiames (08:30–19:00, no booking). Fiames is ~20 min from Cortina, ~1 h 05–15 from Padola. The multi-night versions finish at Passo Falzarego, so plan the car shuffle.

The airy bits, avoided

This routing deliberately skips Alta Via 1's three exposed sections — the Braies entry, Forcella del Lago, and the path under Tofana di Rozes. Draft day distances are estimates; your mom may well know better lines between the huts.

Booking Priority · medium–high

Sennes
sennes.com · dorm €55 / ensuite €75 + dinner
Fanes / Lavarella
lavarella.it · HB €99 dorm / €105 room
Lagazuoi
rifugiolagazuoi.com · ~€115 HB · 50% deposit
Confirm
Rif. Biella 2026 dates/prices by phone; book only the nights your chosen version needs.
The 1-night version books just Sennes; 2-night books Fanes + Lagazuoi; 3-night books Sennes + Fanes + Lagazuoi.
02 Cortina 1 night Busy by day

Cinque Torri: sleep Scoiattoli, sunset at Nuvolauthe classic Dolomite sunset — after everyone leaves

The postcard sunset of the western Dolomites, from Rifugio Nuvolau, the oldest hut in the range (1883). You sleep just below at comfortable Rifugio Scoiattoli (Nuvolau itself is booked solid all summer) and have the amphitheatre to yourselves once the day crowds go down. Two ways to reach it:

Walk
~9–10 km
Ascent
+450–550 m
Time
4–5 h
High point
2 575 m
Lift
Down only (or none)

The walk

Confident

From Passo Giau, take trail 452 west around Ra Gusela to Rifugio Averau (~1½ h, cable-free). Keep LEFT at the Ra Gusela junction — the right branch (trail 438) is the cabled ferrata, not for you. Add the short spur up trail 439 to the Nuvolau summit for the view (optional; a brief hands-on step onto an airy top — turn back if it’s not for you), then down to Scoiattoli via the Cinque Torri circuit to sleep. Don’t descend trail 443 (exposed switchbacks).

Getting there & the car

Start at Passo Giau — ~30 min from Cortina, ~1 h 35–45 from Padola; free car park that fills by ~9 am. You finish at Bai de Dònes (chairlift down), so it’s a one-car shuffle — or make it an out-and-back to Passo Giau (same trails, no shuttle, no lift) if you’d rather keep it simple. Avoid the SP638 on 5 Jul 2026 (Maratona road closure).

Walk
~1 h
Ascent
+320 m
High point
2 575 m
Lift
Chairlift both ways
Effort
Easy evening

The walk

Easy

The short, honest option: ride the chairlift up to Scoiattoli, then a ~1 h stroll up trail 439 past Averau to Nuvolau for the sunset — an easy evening, not a hike. Cable-free footpath with a short hands-on step onto Nuvolau’s airy top; return the way you came, never onto the Ra Gusela ferrata. Chairlift down in the morning.

Getting there

Trailhead Bai de Dònes — ~20–25 min from Cortina, free car park (full by ~9 am). Cinque Torri chairlift 09:00–17:00, ~€27.50 return through 31 Jul, €30 in August (one-way €22.50). It’s the quick option for when you just want the sunset without the day.

The huts

Rifugio Scoiattoli (2,255 m) — your bed either way: 2–5-bed rooms, showers on every floor, a famous wood-fired hot tub (book ahead), half-board ~€85–90. Rifugio Nuvolau (2,575 m) — the sunset objective: austere, dorm-only, no shower, and full for 2026, so you visit it for the light and sleep at Scoiattoli.

Crowds, care & the WWI museum

A honeypot by day — the reason to sleep up here is the empty, glowing evening and dawn. Keep back from Nuvolau’s terrace cliff edge. Five minutes from Scoiattoli is the free Cinque Torri open-air museum — restored WWI trenches and gun positions, an easy wander.

Booking Priority · high

Sleep · Scoiattoli
rifugioscoiattoli.it · +39 0436 867939
Season
Opens 15 Jun 2026
Nuvolau
Full for 2026 — visit for sunset only (watch for cancellations)
Hot tub
Pre-book separately at Scoiattoli
03 Cortina Day or 1 night Moderate Busy — beat it by timing

Lago di Sorapis → Rifugio Vandellithe turquoise lake, with the crowds timed out

That impossible milky-turquoise lake under the Sorapis walls. It's not a hard walk — there's more than one way in — but by day it's one of the busiest trails in the Dolomites. Two ways to play it: sleep on the shore and have it at dusk and dawn, or day-hike it early before the crowds land.

Take your mom's line to the lake

There's more than one route to Sorapis, and the standard one has a short exposed/cabled ledge you don't need. Ask Jimmi's mom which way she'd send you — the quieter, non-cabled line — and follow that. (I'll drop her exact "at X, go…" directions in here once we have them.) Walk in late, out early, and skip it in the wet — the rock is polished and slick.

To the lake
~5.5 km
Ascent
+300–400 m
Time in
~2 h
Lake / hut
1 923 m
Your bed
Vandelli · on the lake

The walk

Moderate

Walk in from Passo Tre Croci — the standard trail 215 is ~5.5 km, +300–400 m, ~2 h (take your mom's line, above, for the quieter way in). Arrive around 4 pm as the day-trippers stream out, and the lake is yours at dusk and dawn. Out early the next morning.

The hut

Rifugio Vandelli (1,923 m) — a CAI hut right beside Lago di Sorapis. Season 20 Jun – 20 Sep 2026. Book ahead; bring cash for extras.

Round trip
~11–12 km
Ascent
+450–600 m
Time
~4–5 h
Lake
1 923 m
Lift
None · on foot

The walk

Moderate

Out and back on trail 215 — ~11–12 km round, +450–600 m, ~4–5 h with time at the lake. Be at Passo Tre Croci by ~7:30 am: it's the only way to find parking and reach the lake ahead of the crowds (300–500 people by midday). Vandelli does drinks and lunch on the shore.

If it's busy

By late morning the lake is mobbed and the car park long full. If you can't start early, this is a strong argument for the overnight instead — or come back another dawn.

Getting there

Trailhead Passo Tre Croci — ~15–20 min from Cortina, ~55–65 min from Padola. Roadside parking fills very early; overnighters arrive late afternoon when spaces free up, day-trippers need to be there by ~7:30 am.

Crowds & care

Genuinely mobbed midday (300–500 people). Sleeping flips it entirely; day-tripping means going early. Either way take your mom's line (above) to avoid the pinch on the standard trail's ledge, and skip it in the wet.

Note

The onward Giro del Sorapis / Ferrata Berti loop has been closed since 2025 for rockfall — the out-and-back to the lake is the plan anyway. Reconfirm before you go.

Booking Priority · medium

Sleep · Vandelli
rifugiovandelli.it · WhatsApp +39 379 2759653 (in-season) · max 1 night
Season
20 Jun – 20 Sep 2026
Day trip
No booking needed — just go early
To confirm
Your mom's exact route to the lake
04 Cortina Day trip Some crowds

Tofana · Ra Vallesnot a hut night — but a spectacular easy day

Worth including, with one honest catch: there are no beds up here — "Capanna Ra Valles" is a day restaurant at the cable-car station (the highest pizza in the Dolomites), not an overnight hut. So this is a day out, not a sleepover: ride the Freccia nel Cielo up into the high Tofane, wander the easy panel-trails at the top, lunch on the terrace, and ride down.

The day

Gondola from Cortina up to Ra Valles (2,475 m). Up top, the short Giulia Ramelli and Permafrost trails (~40–50 min each, "easy, for everyone") give big views with no exposure. Lunch, then ride down.

Why not an overnight

The only walk-ups to Ra Valles are a long scree slog or a cabled path — and there's no bed regardless. If you ever want to sleep in the Tofane, the beginner options are Rifugio Dibona (car-accessible, no lift) or Rifugio Duca d'Aosta (a short chairlift, then easy walking) — a separate trip.

Good to know Day trip

Lift
freccianelcielo.com · Cortina→Ra Valles ~€35 return · 13 Jun–27 Sep 2026
Keep in mind
Stay on the marked panel-trails; everything else up here is via ferrata / scree.
Also worth considering

A few more I turned up

Not family picks — these came out of the research — but they fit your rules (cable-free, quiet-ish, walkable solo) and are worth a look alongside the ones above.

05 Cortina Day or 1 night Moderate Some crowds

Rifugio Croda da Lagoa larch-ringed lake and its reflection

The pretty, restful one — no lifts. A steady forest climb to Lago Federa, a small lake ringed by larches with the Becco di Mezzodì mirrored in it; the hut sits on the shore, loveliest once the day walkers have gone. Keep it a gentle out-and-back, or make a big Confident day of the full loop — sleep at the hut either way.

Walk in
~4.2 km
Ascent
+340 m
Time to hut
~2–2½ h
Hut height
2 046 m
Access
On foot · no lift

The walk

Moderate

Out-and-back to the lake on 437 → 434 — ~4.2 km each way, +340 m, cable-free. Sleep at the hut and have the shore to yourselves at dusk, or do it as an easy day and be back down by mid-afternoon.

Loop
~13 km
Ascent
+820–900 m
Time
~5–6 h
High point
2 460 m
Grade
Confident · dry only

The walk

Confident

Full loop over Forcella de Formin (~13 km, +820–900 m) for a big day — no cables, but a loose boulder descent; go counter-clockwise, dry only, poles for the drop. Best broken up by sleeping at the hut.

The hut

Rifugio Croda da Lago "Palmieri" (2,046 m) — right on Lago Federa, terrace over the water. Opens 15 Jun 2026; confirm rooms/prices direct.

Getting there

Trailhead Ponte de Ru Curto on the SP638 Passo Giau road — ~15 min from Cortina. Roadside parking is limited — before ~9:30 am, or take the Linea 53 bus (Cortina–Passo Giau–Pescul) to the Pian del Pantan stop at the trailhead.

Crowds & care

Popular but nothing like the icons, and quiet by evening. The lake reflection at dawn is the payoff. Poles for the descent.

Booking Priority · relaxed

Book direct
crodadalago.it · +39 0436 862085
Season
Opens 15 Jun 2026 · bring cash
06 Padola Day or 1 night Moderate Quiet ★

Rifugio Fonda-Saviothe quiet amphitheatre of the Cadini

The closest to Padola, and one of the calmest — a natural amphitheatre ringed by the slender spires of the Cadini di Misurina, a stone's throw from the Tre Cime yet a world quieter. One steady climb, no toll, no reservation gymnastics — short enough for a morning, quiet enough to be worth a night.

One small honesty note

Sources split on whether the top of trail 115 has one short secured (cabled) step just below the hut — most call it trivial, passable without touching the cable, but it means it's not strictly 100% cable-free. And turn around at the hut: don't take the cabled 117 loop down, don't continue toward Forcella del Diavolo, and ignore the "Via Ferrata Merlone" signs.

Walk in
~3.5 km
Ascent
+520 m
Time to hut
~1½ h
Hut height
2 367 m
Toll
None · free lot

The walk

Moderate

Up trail 115 — ~3.5 km, +520 m, ~1¼–1¾ h, CAI grade E, no sustained exposure. Walk up in the afternoon and the amphitheatre and its spires are yours at sunset and dawn.

Round trip
~6–7 km
Ascent
+520 m
Time
~3 h
High point
2 367 m
Toll
None · free lot

The walk

Moderate

Up and back on trail 115 — ~6–7 km round, +520 m, ~3 h. Short enough for a morning: climb, lunch on the terrace under the spires, back down for the afternoon. Turn around at the hut (see note above).

The hut

Rifugio Fratelli Fonda-Savio (2,367 m) — friendly and unfussy, deep in the Cadini. Cash only. Season roughly mid-June to end-September — call to confirm 2026.

Getting there

Free Cadini car park by Lago d'Antorno, below the Tre Cime toll gate — no €40 toll. ~45–55 min from Padola. Small lot, fills early.

Crowds & care

Genuinely quiet — far fewer people than anywhere near the Tre Cime next door. Early-season snow can linger in the top gully.

Booking Priority · relaxed

Book direct
fonda-savio.it · +39 0435 39036 · cash only
Season
~15 Jun – 30 Sep (confirm)
07 Cortina 1–2 nights Confident Busy by day

Rifugio Lagazuoi (+ Averau)the great terrace — as a single night or a two-hut traverse

If you don't do the full Fanes traverse, Lagazuoi is worth a night on its own: a hut at 2,752 m with the finest terrace in the range. You walk up (its cable car goes straight to the top, so you save that for the ride down), sleep, and can either head home or carry on to Rifugio Averau for a second night and a celebrated dinner.

Walk up
+647 m
Time up
~2–2½ h
Hut height
2 752 m
Down
Cable car, at the end
Exposure
None

The walk

Confident

Up trails 402 → 401 from Passo Falzarego (~4 km, +647 m, 2–2½ h) — cable-free and non-exposed, but a genuine sustained climb. An upper pitch can hold a snow patch even in July; check the day before. Cable car down in the morning.

Extending to Averau — one airy stretch

The last stretch up to Averau is the crux — a narrow rocky canalino just below Forcella Averau (a brief hands-on scramble, the day's hardest moment), then a giant boulder field and a final traverse under Averau's walls. No cables, officially a grade-E path. No rockfall incidents are documented here, so a helmet is an optional extra, not standard practice — but go in dry weather only and don't linger under the walls. A gentler line via Col Gallina and trail 419 joins 441 lower down if nerves prefer it. At Passo Falzarego, follow 401 — never "Kaiserjäger" (a cabled ferrata off the same start).

Walk up
+647 m
Time up
~2–2½ h
Hut height
2 752 m
Night 2
Averau · 2 413 m
Lift
Down only, at the end

The walk

Confident

Day one as the one-night walk up to Lagazuoi. Day two: down to the pass and up trail 441 to Averau (see the note above), then chairlift down from the Cinque Torri side. A big, high two days with one brief airy stretch — dry weather only.

The hut(s)

Rifugio Lagazuoi (2,752 m) — highest, largest hut above Cortina; dorms + private rooms, showers, larch sauna, the sunset terrace. Rifugio Averau (2,413 m) — the optional second night, famous kitchen, private rooms.

Getting there

Trailhead Passo Falzarego — ~25–30 min from Cortina, ~1 h 25–45 from Padola; car park at the pass by the cable-car station (overnight parking €10/night, not covered by the lift ticket). The Lagazuoi cable car runs if you ever need a bail-out. Note the Maratona dles Dolomites closes the Falzarego road on 5 Jul 2026 until ~14:30.

Booking Priority · medium

Lagazuoi
rifugiolagazuoi.com · ~€115 HB · 50% deposit
Averau (if extending)
rifugioaverau.bukly.com · ~€75–90
Do this part first

Book in this order

Late July and August are the busiest weeks of the Alpine year, and the best huts fill months ahead. It's already July — treat booking as urgent.

  1. Scoiattoli (Cinque Torri)The marquee-sunset bed — and Nuvolau above it is already sold out for 2026, so Scoiattoli goes fast.Phone / online
  2. The Fanes traverse hutsSennes, Fanes/Lavarella, Lagazuoi — three consecutive nights, so lock the chain early. Call Biella to confirm 2026.Online / phone
  3. Rifugio Vandelli (Sorapis)Small lakeside hut, and the overnight is your whole crowd strategy — reserve it.Phone / online
  4. Croda da Lago & Fonda-SavioThe relaxed pair — a week or two out is fine; the constraint is the small car parks, not the beds.Phone / direct

Non-hut bookings not to forget: the €10 Fiames shuttle to Ra Stua (no booking needed, but know the road's shut to cars 6 Jul–6 Sep), and a plan for the car shuffle at the ends of the Fanes traverse and the Lagazuoi→Averau extension.

How this was checked

The featured picks come from your family — first-hand, local, decades of it — and that outranks any website. Around them, because Steve & Leigh walk these unguided, every route was cross-checked against real hiker reviews (AllTrails, Komoot, Outdooractive), guidebooks (Cicerone / Alta Via 1), and the official Italian CAI grades — specifically hunting for cables, exposure, and "got stuck / turned back" reports, and routing around anything that needed them. That's why the Fanes traverse skips three airy Alta Via 1 sections, and why the crowded, exposed Tre Cime overnight was left out.

Honest residuals are flagged in place: Sorapis's ledge (take your mom's line), the one airy stretch on the Lagazuoi→Averau extension, the possible short secured step at Fonda-Savio, and Lagazuoi's snow-prone upper pitch. None is a fixed-cable or fall trap on the route as described — the real risks are loose ground, afternoon thunderstorms, and busy trailheads, all handled by early starts and sleeping over.

Working draft · v3

About this guide

A full-day, cable-free, self-guided guide to overnight rifugi in the Dolomites — a family-picked flagship traverse plus day-hikes, chosen to stay clear of the worst peak-season crowds, from your bases in Padola and Cortina. Built for Steve & Leigh, summer 2026.

Maps: each route's button opens an interactive map (or the location) in a new tab; a fully embedded pannable map can't live inside a shareable page like this.

Please double-check before you go

Dates, timetables, prices and the draft traverse distances come from official and local sources but shift year to year — confirm each when you book (some, marked ~ or "confirm," are estimates). Mountain weather turns fast in the afternoons: start early and be off the tops by early afternoon.
